it sticks out more from the enlcosure. It is 
attached with a Qwiic connecter (JST-SH with I2C).
This is to save cost from it being a seperate PCB.

I used the Arduino MEGA board, therefore, 
the labels in the MCU refer to the 
board's pins: PIN[FUNCTION]. If FUNCTION 
is not specified, it is the default one.
Also note that the RESET is pulled up
inside of the board. The pins that are 
not connected to anything nor marked as
unconnected are the ones that are not 
mapped to any pin on the Arduino board
(but connected internally)

This capacitor is not necessary, the Arduino 
boards reset when the serial connection is 
established. It is a feature, they did it to 
enter the bootloader during the MCU programming, 
but it can cause problems in applications like 
this. In the board there is the possibility to 
cut the little link to stop it from doing that. 
Indead of cutting the link, this capacitor can 
prevent the board from resetting. It should be 
possible to achieve the same result via software 
disabling the DTR, but to make the connection 
easier, I decided to connect the capacitor.
